title: Markdown to HTML renderer with styling for common elements
purpose: Converts markdown content into styled HTML elements with support for headings, code blocks, lists, links, and inline formatting.
module_context: Exports a MarkdownRenderer component that accepts markdown content and optional class name, along with helper functions for rendering markdown and inline formatting.

steps.content: Split the input content into lines for processing.
Initialize an array to collect rendered elements and tracking variables for code blocks.
Process each line to identify and render code blocks, headers, lists, blockquotes, horizontal rules, and paragraphs.
Handle code blocks by detecting opening and closing triple backticks and rendering with appropriate styling.
Render headers with appropriate heading tags and styling based on level.
Process list items with bullet points or numbered lists and apply appropriate styling.
Render blockquotes with appropriate styling and indentation.
Handle horizontal rules by rendering hr elements with styling.
Initialize an array to collect rendered parts and tracking variables.
Process the text to identify and render inline code, links, bold, and italic formatting.
Handle inline code by detecting backticks and rendering with appropriate styling.
Process links by detecting bracket and parentheses patterns and rendering with appropriate styling.
Render remaining text after processing special formatting.
Check for bold formatting by detecting double asterisks and render with strong tag.
IF bold formatting is found, split the text and render parts before, during, and after the bold section.
ELSE check for italic formatting by detecting single asterisks and render with em tag.
IF italic formatting is found, split the text and render parts before, during, and after the italic section.
ELSE return the original text without any formatting.
Check if the input content is empty or contains only whitespace.
IF the content is empty, render a placeholder message with appropriate styling.
ELSE render the markdown content using the renderMarkdown function within a styled article element.
